reference: This type of pain usually occurs due to a fractured rib or a bruise on the rib, and it usually takes a while before going away even if you take regular analgesics. It may take even up to six months. If it becomes too troublesome, get a nerve block from a pain specialist after you get an X-ray of the chest.
text: This type of pain usually occurs due to a fractured rib or a bruise on the rib, and it usually takes a while before going away. Even if you take regular analgesics, it may take even up to six months. If it becomes too troublesome, get a nerve block from a pain specialist after you get an X-ray of the chest.
